47: 
48: ```
49: ALWAYS:    SKILL.md (this file)
50: ON_DEMAND: references/cupt_commands.md    (when cupt command details needed)
51:            references/mcp_tools.md         (when MCP tool details needed)
52:            references/troubleshooting.md   (when error or auth issue detected)
53: ```
54: 
55: ### Operation-to-Tool Routing Table
56: 
57: | Operation | Primary Tool | Command | MCP Fallback |
58: |-----------|-------------|---------|-------------|
59: | List/filter tasks | cupt | `cupt list [--today\|--week\|--tag X]` | `clickup_search_tasks` |
60: | Task details | cupt | `cupt show <id> [--notes]` | `clickup_get_task` |
61: | Mark task complete | cupt | `cupt done <id> [--dry-run]` | `clickup_update_task` |
62: | Add note/comment | cupt | `cupt note <id> "<text>"` | `clickup_manage_comments` |
63: | Read comments | cupt | `cupt notes <id>` | `clickup_manage_comments` |
64: | Start/stop timer | cupt | `cupt time start <id>` / `cupt time stop` | MCP time tracking |
65: | Log time manually | cupt | `cupt time add <id> <dur>` | MCP time tracking |
66: | Add tag | cupt | `cupt tag add <id> <name>` | `clickup_add_tag_to_task` |
67: | Remove tag | cupt | `cupt tag remove <id> <name>` | `clickup_remove_tag_from_task` |
68: | Task context | cupt | `cupt context <id>` | n/a |
69: | Discover statuses | cupt | `cupt statuses <id>` | n/a |
70: | **Documents** | **MCP only** | n/a | `clickup_create_document` |
71: | **Goals/OKRs** | **MCP only** | n/a | goal management tools |
72: | **Bulk create 5+** | **MCP only** | n/a | `clickup_create_bulk_tasks` |
73: | **Webhooks** | **MCP only** | n/a | webhook management tools |
74: | **Chat** | **MCP only** | n/a | chat tools |
75: | **Audit logs** | **MCP only** | n/a | `clickup_get_audit_logs` |
76: 
77: ### Smart Router Pseudocode
78: 
79: ```python
80: # Intent signals with weights
81: INTENT_SIGNALS = {
82:     "CUPT_DAILY": {
83:         "weight": 5,
84:         "keywords": ["list", "show", "done", "note", "notes", "time", "tag", "context",
85:                      "statuses", "summary", "teams", "attach", "work queue", "complete task",
86:                      "mark done", "log time", "track time"],
87:     },
88:     "MCP_ADVANCED": {
89:         "weight": 5,
90:         "keywords": ["document", "goal", "okr", "bulk", "webhook", "chat", "audit",
91:                      "create_bulk", "manage_documents", "checklist", "custom field"],
92:     },
93:     "INSTALL": {
94:         "weight": 6,
95:         "keywords": ["install cupt", "setup", "not found", "not installed", "auth",
96:                      "authenticate", "api token", "mcp config"],
97:     },
98:     "TROUBLESHOOT": {
99:         "weight": 6,
100:         "keywords": ["error", "failed", "not working", "403", "401", "slow", "timeout",
101:                      "empty", "no tasks"],
102:     },
103: }
104: 
105: RESOURCE_MAP = {
106:     "CUPT_DAILY":    ["references/cupt_commands.md"],
107:     "MCP_ADVANCED":  ["references/mcp_tools.md"],
108:     "INSTALL":       ["INSTALL_GUIDE.md", "scripts/install.sh"],
109:     "TROUBLESHOOT":  ["references/troubleshooting.md"],
110:     "DEFAULT":       ["references/cupt_commands.md"],
111: }
112: 
113: def route_clickup_resources(request: str) -> list[str]:
114:     """Score intents, load matching reference files."""
115:     request_lower = request.lower()
116: 
117:     scores = {}
118:     for intent, config in INTENT_SIGNALS.items():
119:         score = sum(
120:             config["weight"] for kw in config["keywords"]
121:             if kw in request_lower
122:         )
123:         if score > 0:
124:             scores[intent] = score
125: 
126:     if not scores:
127:         return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP["DEFAULT"])
128: 
129:     # Error/install keywords boost TROUBLESHOOT/INSTALL regardless of other signals
130:     if scores.get("TROUBLESHOOT", 0) > 3:
131:         return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP["TROUBLESHOOT"])
132:     if scores.get("INSTALL", 0) > 4:
133:         return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP["INSTALL"])
134: 
135:     # Pick top intent
136:     top_intent = max(scores, key=scores.get)
137:     return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP[top_intent])
138: 
139: def load_resources(resource_list: list[str]) -> list[str]:
140:     """Load resource files, guard to skill directory."""
141:     skill_root = ".opencode/skills/mcp-click-up/"
142:     loaded = []
143:     for resource in resource_list:
144:         path = skill_root + resource
145:         if _guard_in_skill(path):
146:             loaded.append(Read(path))
147:     return loaded
148: ```
149: 
150: ---
151: 
152: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS
153: 
154: ### Tool Comparison
155: 
156: | Dimension | cupt CLI | Official ClickUp MCP |
157: |-----------|---------|---------------------|
158: | **Activation** | `cupt <command>` in Bash | Code Mode `call_tool_chain()` |
159: | **Best for** | Daily task ops, time tracking, notes, tags | Documents, goals, bulk ops, webhooks |
160: | **Output** | Human-readable + `--json` flag | Structured JSON always |
161: | **Auth** | `cupt auth` / `cupt config --api-token` | `CLICKUP_API_KEY` env var |
162: | **Offline** | `--offline` flag uses local cache | Always requires network |
163: | **Install** | `pipx install cupt` (Python) | `npx @clickup/mcp-server` (Node) |
164: | **Dry-run** | `cupt done --dry-run` | No equivalent |
165: | **Status auto** | Yes — resolves per-list | No — must specify status |
166: 
167: ### cupt CLI (Primary Path)
168: 
169: **Step 1: Verify installation**
170: ```bash
171: cupt --version   # e.g. cupt 0.7.1

175: **Step 2: Install if missing**
176: ```bash
177: bash .opencode/skills/mcp-click-up/scripts/install.sh
178: ```
179: 
180: **Step 3: Authenticate**
181: ```bash
182: cupt auth                          # Interactive wizard
183: # OR:
184: cupt config --api-token pk_xxxxx   # Direct token setup
185: ```
186: 
187: **Step 4: Set defaults (optional)**
188: ```bash
189: cupt config --workspace-id <id>    # From cupt status
190: cupt config --default-list <id>    # For quick task creation
191: ```
192: 
193: **Step 5: Use for daily operations**
194: ```bash
195: cupt list --today --json           # Tasks due today, JSON output
196: cupt statuses <task_id>            # Discover list's status schema FIRST
197: cupt done <task_id> --dry-run      # Preview completion without writing
198: cupt done <task_id> --note "done"  # Mark complete with note
199: ```
200: 
201: ### Official ClickUp MCP (Secondary Path)
202: 
203: **Prerequisites:**
204: - Code Mode MCP configured in opencode.json (see INSTALL_GUIDE.md)
205: - `CLICKUP_API_KEY` and `CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` set in env block
206:

213:     "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "pk_YOUR_TOKEN",

215:   }
216: }
217: ```

# mcp-click-up Skill

ClickUp task management via **cupt CLI** (primary) and **official ClickUp MCP** (secondary). Operation-based routing: cupt handles daily task ops, MCP handles documents, goals, and bulk operations.

---

## 1. WHEN TO USE

### Activation Triggers (explicit user phrases)

- "clickup", "cupt", "mcp-click-up", "click up"
- "manage tasks", "list tasks", "mark task done", "complete task"
- "log time", "track time", "start timer", "stop timer"
- "add note to task", "tag task", "show task details"
- "process work queue", "process task queue"
- "clickup documents", "clickup goals", "bulk create tasks"

### Automatic Triggers (keyword patterns)

- `cupt` appears in the request
- `clickup` + any action verb (list, show, done, mark, note, time, tag)
- "work queue" or "task queue" in a ClickUp context
- "time tracking" or "work log" with project management context
- MCP tool names: `clickup_create_task`, `clickup_get_task`, `clickup_manage_documents`

### When NOT to Use

- **@krodak/clickup-cli (`cu` command)** — different tool entirely; not supported by this skill
- Community ClickUp MCP servers (@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server) — use official MCP only
- Direct ClickUp REST API calls (no CLI/MCP) — this skill adds no value there
- Browser-based ClickUp automation (Playwright/Puppeteer) — wrong surface

---

## 2. SMART ROUTING

### Resource Loading Levels

```
ALWAYS:    SKILL.md (this file)
ON_DEMAND: references/cupt_commands.md    (when cupt command details needed)
           references/mcp_tools.md         (when MCP tool details needed)
           references/troubleshooting.md   (when error or auth issue detected)
```

### Operation-to-Tool Routing Table

| Operation | Primary Tool | Command | MCP Fallback |
|-----------|-------------|---------|-------------|
| List/filter tasks | cupt | `cupt list [--today\|--week\|--tag X]` | `clickup_search_tasks` |
| Task details | cupt | `cupt show <id> [--notes]` | `clickup_get_task` |
| Mark task complete | cupt | `cupt done <id> [--dry-run]` | `clickup_update_task` |
| Add note/comment | cupt | `cupt note <id> "<text>"` | `clickup_manage_comments` |
| Read comments | cupt | `cupt notes <id>` | `clickup_manage_comments` |
| Start/stop timer | cupt | `cupt time start <id>` / `cupt time stop` | MCP time tracking |
| Log time manually | cupt | `cupt time add <id> <dur>` | MCP time tracking |
| Add tag | cupt | `cupt tag add <id> <name>` | `clickup_add_tag_to_task` |
| Remove tag | cupt | `cupt tag remove <id> <name>` | `clickup_remove_tag_from_task` |
| Task context | cupt | `cupt context <id>` | n/a |
| Discover statuses | cupt | `cupt statuses <id>` | n/a |
| **Documents** | **MCP only** | n/a | `clickup_create_document` |
| **Goals/OKRs** | **MCP only** | n/a | goal management tools |
| **Bulk create 5+** | **MCP only** | n/a | `clickup_create_bulk_tasks` |
| **Webhooks** | **MCP only** | n/a | webhook management tools |
| **Chat** | **MCP only** | n/a | chat tools |
| **Audit logs** | **MCP only** | n/a | `clickup_get_audit_logs` |

### Smart Router Pseudocode

```python
# Intent signals with weights
INTENT_SIGNALS = {
    "CUPT_DAILY": {
        "weight": 5,
        "keywords": ["list", "show", "done", "note", "notes", "time", "tag", "context",
                     "statuses", "summary", "teams", "attach", "work queue", "complete task",
                     "mark done", "log time", "track time"],
    },
    "MCP_ADVANCED": {
        "weight": 5,
        "keywords": ["document", "goal", "okr", "bulk", "webhook", "chat", "audit",
                     "create_bulk", "manage_documents", "checklist", "custom field"],
    },
    "INSTALL": {
        "weight": 6,
        "keywords": ["install cupt", "setup", "not found", "not installed", "auth",
                     "authenticate", "api token", "mcp config"],
    },
    "TROUBLESHOOT": {
        "weight": 6,
        "keywords": ["error", "failed", "not working", "403", "401", "slow", "timeout",
                     "empty", "no tasks"],
    },
}

RESOURCE_MAP = {
    "CUPT_DAILY":    ["references/cupt_commands.md"],
    "MCP_ADVANCED":  ["references/mcp_tools.md"],
    "INSTALL":       ["INSTALL_GUIDE.md", "scripts/install.sh"],
    "TROUBLESHOOT":  ["references/troubleshooting.md"],
    "DEFAULT":       ["references/cupt_commands.md"],
}

def route_clickup_resources(request: str) -> list[str]:
    """Score intents, load matching reference files."""
    request_lower = request.lower()

    scores = {}
    for intent, config in INTENT_SIGNALS.items():
        score = sum(
            config["weight"] for kw in config["keywords"]
            if kw in request_lower
        )
        if score > 0:
            scores[intent] = score

    if not scores:
        return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP["DEFAULT"])

    # Error/install keywords boost TROUBLESHOOT/INSTALL regardless of other signals
    if scores.get("TROUBLESHOOT", 0) > 3:
        return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP["TROUBLESHOOT"])
    if scores.get("INSTALL", 0) > 4:
        return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP["INSTALL"])

    # Pick top intent
    top_intent = max(scores, key=scores.get)
    return load_resources(RESOURCE_MAP[top_intent])

def load_resources(resource_list: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Load resource files, guard to skill directory."""
    skill_root = ".opencode/skills/mcp-click-up/"
    loaded = []
    for resource in resource_list:
        path = skill_root + resource
        if _guard_in_skill(path):
            loaded.append(Read(path))
    return loaded
```

---

## 3. HOW IT WORKS

### Tool Comparison

| Dimension | cupt CLI | Official ClickUp MCP |
|-----------|---------|---------------------|
| **Activation** | `cupt <command>` in Bash | Code Mode `call_tool_chain()` |
| **Best for** | Daily task ops, time tracking, notes, tags | Documents, goals, bulk ops, webhooks |
| **Output** | Human-readable + `--json` flag | Structured JSON always |
| **Auth** | `cupt auth` / `cupt config --api-token` | `CLICKUP_API_KEY` env var |
| **Offline** | `--offline` flag uses local cache | Always requires network |
| **Install** | `pipx install cupt` (Python) | `npx @clickup/mcp-server` (Node) |
| **Dry-run** | `cupt done --dry-run` | No equivalent |
| **Status auto** | Yes — resolves per-list | No — must specify status |

### cupt CLI (Primary Path)

**Step 1: Verify installation**
```bash
cupt --version   # e.g. cupt 0.7.1
cupt status      # Shows workspace + auth status
```

**Step 2: Install if missing**
```bash
bash .opencode/skills/mcp-click-up/scripts/install.sh
```

**Step 3: Authenticate**
```bash
cupt auth                          # Interactive wizard
# OR:
cupt config --api-token pk_xxxxx   # Direct token setup
```

**Step 4: Set defaults (optional)**
```bash
cupt config --workspace-id <id>    # From cupt status
cupt config --default-list <id>    # For quick task creation
```

**Step 5: Use for daily operations**
```bash
cupt list --today --json           # Tasks due today, JSON output
cupt statuses <task_id>            # Discover list's status schema FIRST
cupt done <task_id> --dry-run      # Preview completion without writing
cupt done <task_id> --note "done"  # Mark complete with note
```

### Official ClickUp MCP (Secondary Path)

**Prerequisites:**
- Code Mode MCP configured in opencode.json (see INSTALL_GUIDE.md)
- `CLICKUP_API_KEY` and `CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` set in env block

**Configuration** (add to `opencode.json` mcpServers):
```json
"clickup": {
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "@clickup/mcp-server"],
  "env": {
    "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "pk_YOUR_TOKEN",
    "CLICKUP_TEAM_ID": "YOUR_WORKSPACE_ID"
  }
}
```

**Invocation via Code Mode:**

10: # MCP Code Mode
11: 
12: Execute TypeScript code with direct access to 200+ MCP tools through progressive disclosure. Code Mode eliminates context overhead by loading tools on-demand, enabling complex multi-tool workflows in a single execution with state persistence and built-in error handling.
13: 
14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE
15: 
16: ### Use Code Mode When
17: 
18: **MANDATORY for ALL MCP tool calls**:
19: - ✅ Calling ClickUp, Notion, Figma, MyService, Chrome DevTools, or any other MCP tools
20: - ✅ Accessing external APIs through MCP servers
21: - ✅ Managing tasks in project management tools
22: - ✅ Interacting with design tools, databases, or services
23: - ✅ Browser automation and web interactions
24: 
25: **Benefits over traditional tool calling**:
26: - 🚀 **98.7% context reduction** - 1.6k tokens vs 141k for 47 tools
27: - ⚡ **60% faster execution** - Single execution vs 15+ API round trips
28: - 🔗 **State persistence** - Data flows naturally between operations
29: - 🛡️ **Type safety** - Full TypeScript support with autocomplete
30: - 🎯 **Progressive loading** - Tools discovered on-demand, zero upfront cost
31: 
32: ### Do NOT Use Code Mode For
33: 
34: **Use native tools instead**:
35: - ❌ File operations (use Read, Write, Edit tools)
36: - ❌ Text searching (use Grep tool)
37: - ❌ File discovery (use Glob tool)
38: - ❌ Bash commands (use Bash tool)
39: - ❌ Continuity recovery (use `/speckit:resume` first; only use native Spec Kit Memory tools after `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs` has been exhausted)
40: - ❌ Sequential Thinking (call `sequential_thinking_sequentialthinking()` directly - **NATIVE MCP**)
41: 
42: > See Section 4 for details on Native MCP vs Code Mode distinction.
43: 
44: ### Common Use Cases
45: 
46: | Scenario                     | Code Mode Approach                                                      | Benefit                     |
47: | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
48: | **Create ClickUp task**      | `call_tool_chain({ code: "await clickup.clickup_create_task({...})" })` | Type-safe, single execution |
49: | **Multi-tool workflow**      | Figma → ClickUp → MyService in one execution                              | State persists, 5× faster   |
50: | **Browser automation**       | Chrome DevTools MCP for testing/screenshots                             | Sandboxed, reliable         |
51: | **Design-to-implementation** | Fetch Figma design → Create task → Update CMS                           | Atomic workflow             |
52: | **External API access**      | Any MCP server (Notion, GitHub, etc.)                                   | Progressive tool loading    |
53: 
54: ---
55: 
56: ## 2. SMART ROUTING
57: 
58: 
59: ### Resource Loading Levels
60: 
61: | Level       | When to Load             | Resources                    |
62: | ----------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------- |
63: | ALWAYS      | Every skill invocation   | Core quick reference         |
64: | CONDITIONAL | If intent signals match  | Intent-mapped references     |
65: | ON_DEMAND   | Only on explicit request | Full configuration/workflows |
66: 
67: ### Smart Router Pseudocode
68: 
69: The authoritative routing logic for scoped loading, weighted intent scoring, and ambiguity handling.
70: 
71: - Pattern 1: Runtime Discovery - `discover_markdown_resources()` recursively inventories `references/` and `assets/`.
72: - Pattern 2: Existence-Check Before Load - `load_if_available()` guards paths, checks `inventory`, and de-duplicates with `seen`.
73: - Pattern 3: Extensible Routing Key - intent and command signals select Code Mode routing labels.
74: - Pattern 4: Multi-Tier Graceful Fallback - `UNKNOWN_FALLBACK` returns a disambiguation checklist and missing matches return a "no knowledge base" notice.
75: 
76: ```python
77: from pathlib import Path
78: 
79: SKILL_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
80: RESOURCE_BASES = (SKILL_ROOT / "references", SKILL_ROOT / "assets")
81: DEFAULT_RESOURCE = "references/workflows.md"
82: 
83: INTENT_SIGNALS = {
84:     "NAMING": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["tool not found", "naming", "prefix", "format"]},
85:     "SETUP": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["setup", "install", "configure", ".utcp_config", ".env"]},
86:     "VALIDATE": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["validate", "validation", "check config", "schema"]},
87:     "CATALOG": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["what tools", "list tools", "discover tools", "catalog"]},
88:     "WORKFLOW": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["workflow", "orchestrate", "multi-tool", "error handling"]},
89:     "ARCHITECTURE": {"weight": 2, "keywords": ["architecture", "token", "performance", "internals"]},
90: }
91: 
92: RESOURCE_MAP = {
93:     "NAMING": ["references/naming_convention.md"],
94:     "SETUP": ["references/configuration.md", "assets/config_template.md", "assets/env_template.md"],
95:     "VALIDATE": ["references/configuration.md", "references/naming_convention.md"],
96:     "CATALOG": ["references/tool_catalog.md"],
97:     "WORKFLOW": ["references/workflows.md"],
98:     "ARCHITECTURE": ["references/architecture.md"],
99: }
100: 
101: COMMAND_BOOSTS = {
102:     "search_tools": "CATALOG",
103:     "list_tools": "CATALOG",
104:     "tool_info": "CATALOG",
105:     "call_tool_chain": "WORKFLOW",
106: }
107: 
108: LOADING_LEVELS = {
109:     "ALWAYS": [DEFAULT_RESOURCE],
110:     "ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS": ["full config", "deep dive", "full workflow", "all tools", "call_tool_chain", "tool chain", "mcp tools", "tool catalog", "code mode"],
111:     "ON_DEMAND": ["references/configuration.md", "references/workflows.md"],
112: }
113: 
114: def _task_text(task) -> str:
115:     parts = [
116:         str(getattr(task, "query", "")),
117:         str(getattr(task, "text", "")),
118:         " ".join(getattr(task, "keywords", []) or []),
119:         str(getattr(task, "command", "")),
120:     ]
121:     return " ".join(parts).lower()
122: 
123: def _guard_in_skill(relative_path: str) -> str:
124:     resolved = (SKILL_ROOT / relative_path).resolve()
125:     resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT)
126:     if resolved.suffix.lower() != ".md":
127:         raise ValueError(f"Only markdown resources are routable: {relative_path}")
128:     return resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix()
129: 
130: def discover_markdown_resources() -> set[str]:
131:     docs = []
132:     for base in RESOURCE_BASES:
133:         if base.exists():
134:             docs.extend(p for p in base.rglob("*.md") if p.is_file())
135:     return {doc.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix() for doc in docs}
136: 
137: def score_intents(task) -> dict[str, float]:
138:     """Weighted intent scoring from request text and signals."""
139:     text = _task_text(task)
140:     scores = {intent: 0.0 for intent in INTENT_SIGNALS}
141:     for intent, cfg in INTENT_SIGNALS.items():
142:         for keyword in cfg["keywords"]:
143:             if keyword in text:
144:                 scores[intent] += cfg["weight"]
145:     command = str(getattr(task, "command", "")).lower()
146:     for signal, intent in COMMAND_BOOSTS.items():
147:         if signal in command:
148:             scores[intent] += 4
149:     return scores
150: 
151: def select_intents(scores: dict[str, float], ambiguity_delta: float = 1.0, max_intents: int = 2) -> list[str]:
152:     ranked = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda item: item[1], reverse=True)
153:     if not ranked or ranked[0][1] <= 0:
154:         return ["WORKFLOW"]
155:     selected = [ranked[0][0]]
156:     if len(ranked) > 1 and ranked[1][1] > 0 and (ranked[0][1] - ranked[1][1]) <= ambiguity_delta:
157:         selected.append(ranked[1][0])
158:     return selected[:max_intents]
159: 
160: def route_code_mode_resources(task):
161:     inventory = discover_markdown_resources()
162:     scores = score_intents(task)
163:     intents = select_intents(scores, ambiguity_delta=1.0)
164:     loaded = []
165:     seen = set()
166: 
167:     def load_if_available(relative_path: str) -> None:
168:         guarded = _guard_in_skill(relative_path)
169:         if guarded in inventory and guarded not in seen:
170:             load(guarded)
171:             loaded.append(guarded)
172:             seen.add(guarded)
173: 
174:     for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ALWAYS"]:
175:         load_if_available(relative_path)
176: 
177:     if max(scores.values() or [0]) < 0.5:
178:         return {
179:             "routing_key": "code-mode",
180:             "intents": intents,
181:             "intent_scores": scores,
182:             "load_level": "UNKNOWN_FALLBACK",
183:             "needs_disambiguation": True,
184:             "disambiguation_checklist": ["Confirm tool discovery, setup, validation, or workflow need", "Confirm target MCP/tool family", "Provide the tool call or error if available"],
185:             "resources": loaded,
186:         }
187: 
188:     matched_intents = []
189:     for intent in intents:
190:         before_count = len(loaded)
191:         for relative_path in RESOURCE_MAP.get(intent, []):
192:             load_if_available(relative_path)
193:         if len(loaded) > before_count:
194:             matched_intents.append(intent)
195: 
196:     text = _task_text(task)
197:     if any(keyword in text for keyword in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS"]):
198:         for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND"]:
199:             load_if_available(relative_path)
200: 
201:     if not loaded:
202:         load_if_available(DEFAULT_RESOURCE)
203: 
204:     result = {"routing_key": "code-mode", "intents": intents, "intent_scores": scores, "resources": loaded}
205:     if not matched_intents:
206:         result["notice"] = f"No knowledge base found for intent(s): {', '.join(intents)}"
207:     return result
208: ```
209: 
210: ---
211:

213:

215:

217:

219: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}

# MCP Code Mode

Execute TypeScript code with direct access to 200+ MCP tools through progressive disclosure. Code Mode eliminates context overhead by loading tools on-demand, enabling complex multi-tool workflows in a single execution with state persistence and built-in error handling.

## 1. WHEN TO USE

### Use Code Mode When

**MANDATORY for ALL MCP tool calls**:
- ✅ Calling ClickUp, Notion, Figma, MyService, Chrome DevTools, or any other MCP tools
- ✅ Accessing external APIs through MCP servers
- ✅ Managing tasks in project management tools
- ✅ Interacting with design tools, databases, or services
- ✅ Browser automation and web interactions

**Benefits over traditional tool calling**:
- 🚀 **98.7% context reduction** - 1.6k tokens vs 141k for 47 tools
- ⚡ **60% faster execution** - Single execution vs 15+ API round trips
- 🔗 **State persistence** - Data flows naturally between operations
- 🛡️ **Type safety** - Full TypeScript support with autocomplete
- 🎯 **Progressive loading** - Tools discovered on-demand, zero upfront cost

### Do NOT Use Code Mode For

**Use native tools instead**:
- ❌ File operations (use Read, Write, Edit tools)
- ❌ Text searching (use Grep tool)
- ❌ File discovery (use Glob tool)
- ❌ Bash commands (use Bash tool)
- ❌ Continuity recovery (use `/speckit:resume` first; only use native Spec Kit Memory tools after `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs` has been exhausted)
- ❌ Sequential Thinking (call `sequential_thinking_sequentialthinking()` directly - **NATIVE MCP**)

> See Section 4 for details on Native MCP vs Code Mode distinction.

### Common Use Cases

| Scenario                     | Code Mode Approach                                                      | Benefit                     |
| ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| **Create ClickUp task**      | `call_tool_chain({ code: "await clickup.clickup_create_task({...})" })` | Type-safe, single execution |
| **Multi-tool workflow**      | Figma → ClickUp → MyService in one execution                              | State persists, 5× faster   |
| **Browser automation**       | Chrome DevTools MCP for testing/screenshots                             | Sandboxed, reliable         |
| **Design-to-implementation** | Fetch Figma design → Create task → Update CMS                           | Atomic workflow             |
| **External API access**      | Any MCP server (Notion, GitHub, etc.)                                   | Progressive tool loading    |

---

## 2. SMART ROUTING


### Resource Loading Levels

| Level       | When to Load             | Resources                    |
| ----------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------- |
| ALWAYS      | Every skill invocation   | Core quick reference         |
| CONDITIONAL | If intent signals match  | Intent-mapped references     |
| ON_DEMAND   | Only on explicit request | Full configuration/workflows |

### Smart Router Pseudocode

The authoritative routing logic for scoped loading, weighted intent scoring, and ambiguity handling.

- Pattern 1: Runtime Discovery - `discover_markdown_resources()` recursively inventories `references/` and `assets/`.
- Pattern 2: Existence-Check Before Load - `load_if_available()` guards paths, checks `inventory`, and de-duplicates with `seen`.
- Pattern 3: Extensible Routing Key - intent and command signals select Code Mode routing labels.
- Pattern 4: Multi-Tier Graceful Fallback - `UNKNOWN_FALLBACK` returns a disambiguation checklist and missing matches return a "no knowledge base" notice.

```python
from pathlib import Path

SKILL_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
RESOURCE_BASES = (SKILL_ROOT / "references", SKILL_ROOT / "assets")
DEFAULT_RESOURCE = "references/workflows.md"

INTENT_SIGNALS = {
    "NAMING": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["tool not found", "naming", "prefix", "format"]},
    "SETUP": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["setup", "install", "configure", ".utcp_config", ".env"]},
    "VALIDATE": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["validate", "validation", "check config", "schema"]},
    "CATALOG": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["what tools", "list tools", "discover tools", "catalog"]},
    "WORKFLOW": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["workflow", "orchestrate", "multi-tool", "error handling"]},
    "ARCHITECTURE": {"weight": 2, "keywords": ["architecture", "token", "performance", "internals"]},
}

RESOURCE_MAP = {
    "NAMING": ["references/naming_convention.md"],
    "SETUP": ["references/configuration.md", "assets/config_template.md", "assets/env_template.md"],
    "VALIDATE": ["references/configuration.md", "references/naming_convention.md"],
    "CATALOG": ["references/tool_catalog.md"],
    "WORKFLOW": ["references/workflows.md"],
    "ARCHITECTURE": ["references/architecture.md"],
}

COMMAND_BOOSTS = {
    "search_tools": "CATALOG",
    "list_tools": "CATALOG",
    "tool_info": "CATALOG",
    "call_tool_chain": "WORKFLOW",
}

LOADING_LEVELS = {
    "ALWAYS": [DEFAULT_RESOURCE],
    "ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS": ["full config", "deep dive", "full workflow", "all tools", "call_tool_chain", "tool chain", "mcp tools", "tool catalog", "code mode"],
    "ON_DEMAND": ["references/configuration.md", "references/workflows.md"],
}

def _task_text(task) -> str:
    parts = [
        str(getattr(task, "query", "")),
        str(getattr(task, "text", "")),
        " ".join(getattr(task, "keywords", []) or []),
        str(getattr(task, "command", "")),
    ]
    return " ".join(parts).lower()

def _guard_in_skill(relative_path: str) -> str:
    resolved = (SKILL_ROOT / relative_path).resolve()
    resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT)
    if resolved.suffix.lower() != ".md":
        raise ValueError(f"Only markdown resources are routable: {relative_path}")
    return resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix()

def discover_markdown_resources() -> set[str]:
    docs = []
    for base in RESOURCE_BASES:
        if base.exists():
            docs.extend(p for p in base.rglob("*.md") if p.is_file())
    return {doc.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix() for doc in docs}

def score_intents(task) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Weighted intent scoring from request text and signals."""
    text = _task_text(task)
    scores = {intent: 0.0 for intent in INTENT_SIGNALS}
    for intent, cfg in INTENT_SIGNALS.items():
        for keyword in cfg["keywords"]:
            if keyword in text:
                scores[intent] += cfg["weight"]
    command = str(getattr(task, "command", "")).lower()
    for signal, intent in COMMAND_BOOSTS.items():
        if signal in command:
            scores[intent] += 4
    return scores

def select_intents(scores: dict[str, float], ambiguity_delta: float = 1.0, max_intents: int = 2) -> list[str]:
    ranked = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda item: item[1], reverse=True)
    if not ranked or ranked[0][1] <= 0:
        return ["WORKFLOW"]
    selected = [ranked[0][0]]
    if len(ranked) > 1 and ranked[1][1] > 0 and (ranked[0][1] - ranked[1][1]) <= ambiguity_delta:
        selected.append(ranked[1][0])
    return selected[:max_intents]

def route_code_mode_resources(task):
    inventory = discover_markdown_resources()
    scores = score_intents(task)
    intents = select_intents(scores, ambiguity_delta=1.0)
    loaded = []
    seen = set()

    def load_if_available(relative_path: str) -> None:
        guarded = _guard_in_skill(relative_path)
        if guarded in inventory and guarded not in seen:
            load(guarded)
            loaded.append(guarded)
            seen.add(guarded)

    for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ALWAYS"]:
        load_if_available(relative_path)

    if max(scores.values() or [0]) < 0.5:
        return {
            "routing_key": "code-mode",
            "intents": intents,
            "intent_scores": scores,
            "load_level": "UNKNOWN_FALLBACK",
            "needs_disambiguation": True,
            "disambiguation_checklist": ["Confirm tool discovery, setup, validation, or workflow need", "Confirm target MCP/tool family", "Provide the tool call or error if available"],
            "resources": loaded,
        }

    matched_intents = []
    for intent in intents:
        before_count = len(loaded)
        for relative_path in RESOURCE_MAP.get(intent, []):
            load_if_available(relative_path)
        if len(loaded) > before_count:
            matched_intents.append(intent)

    text = _task_text(task)
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS"]):
        for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND"]:
            load_if_available(relative_path)

    if not loaded:
        load_if_available(DEFAULT_RESOURCE)

    result = {"routing_key": "code-mode", "intents": intents, "intent_scores": scores, "resources": loaded}
    if not matched_intents:
        result["notice"] = f"No knowledge base found for intent(s): {', '.join(intents)}"
    return result
```

---

## 3. HOW IT WORKS

### Critical Naming Pattern

**The #1 most common error** when using Code Mode is using wrong function names. All MCP tool calls MUST follow this pattern:

```typescript
{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}
```

<content>
214: ### Critical Naming Pattern
215: 
216: **The #1 most common error** when using Code Mode is using wrong function names. All MCP tool calls MUST follow this pattern:
217: 
218: ```typescript
219: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}
220: ```
221: 
222: **Examples**:
223: 
224: ✅ **Correct**:
225: ```typescript
226: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
227: await clickup.clickup_create_task({...});
228: await figma.figma_get_file({...});
229: ```
230: 
231: ❌ **Wrong** (missing manual prefix):
232: ```typescript
233: await myservice.sites_list({});        // Error: Tool not found
234: await clickup.create_task({...});    // Error: Tool not found
235: ```
236: 
237: **See [references/naming_convention.md](references/naming_convention.md) for complete guide with troubleshooting.**
238: 
239: ### Context Parameter
240: 
241: Many Code Mode tools require a `context` parameter (15-25 words) for analytics:
242: 
243: ```typescript
244: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({
245:   context: "Listing sites to identify collection structure for CMS update"
246: });
247: ```
248: 
249: This helps with usage tracking and debugging.
250: 
251: ### Tool Name Translation
252: 
253: > **Note:** `list_tools()` returns names in `a.b.c` format (e.g., `myservice.myservice.sites_list`). To call the tool, use underscore format: `myservice.myservice_sites_list()`. The `tool_info()` function shows the correct calling syntax.
254: 
255: ### Basic Workflow
256: 
257: 1. Discover tools with `search_tools()` or `list_tools()`.
258: 2. Confirm exact callable syntax with `tool_info()`.
259: 3. Execute `call_tool_chain()` with `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}` calls.
260: 4. Return structured state from the TypeScript block.
261: 
262: Full multi-tool examples live in [references/workflows.md](references/workflows.md).
263: 
264: ---
265: 
266: ## 4. PROJECT CONFIGURATION
267: 
268: ### Two MCP Configuration Systems
269: 
270: **IMPORTANT**: Code Mode only accesses tools in `.utcp_config.json`. Native MCP tools are NOT accessed through Code Mode.
271: 
272: **1. Native MCP** (`opencode.json`) - Direct tools (call directly, NOT through Code Mode):
273: - **Sequential Thinking**: `sequential_thinking_sequentialthinking()`
274: - **Spec Kit continuity + memory**: `/speckit:resume` for recovery; native Spec Kit Memory tools such as `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, `memory_context()`, and `memory_search()` when canonical packet sources need deeper support
275: - **Code Mode server**: The Code Mode tool itself
276: - **Note**: Some AI environments have built-in extended thinking capabilities that may supersede Sequential Thinking MCP.
277: 
278: **2. Code Mode MCP** (`.utcp_config.json`) - External tools accessed through Code Mode:
279: - **MCP Config**: `.utcp_config.json` (project root)
280: - **Environment Variables**: `.env` (project root)
281: - **External tools**: MyService, Figma, Chrome DevTools, ClickUp, Notion, etc.
282: - These are accessed via `call_tool_chain()` wrapper
283: 
284: ### How to Discover Available Code Mode Tools
285: 
286: **These discovery methods ONLY work for Code Mode tools in `.utcp_config.json`**
287: **They do NOT show Sequential Thinking (which is in `.mcp.json`)**
288: 
289: **Step 1: Check Configuration**
290: ```typescript
291: // Read .utcp_config.json to see configured Code Mode MCP servers
292: // Look for "manual_call_templates" array
293: // Each object has a "name" field (this is the manual name)

### Critical Naming Pattern

**The #1 most common error** when using Code Mode is using wrong function names. All MCP tool calls MUST follow this pattern:

```typescript
{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}
```

**Examples**:

✅ **Correct**:
```typescript
await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
await clickup.clickup_create_task({...});
await figma.figma_get_file({...});
```

❌ **Wrong** (missing manual prefix):
```typescript
await myservice.sites_list({});        // Error: Tool not found

display.text:
### Critical Naming Pattern

**The #1 most common error** when using Code Mode is using wrong function names. All MCP tool calls MUST follow this pattern:

```typescript
{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}
```

**Examples**:

✅ **Correct**:
```typescript
await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
await clickup.clickup_create_task({...});
await figma.figma_get_file({...});
```

❌ **Wrong** (missing manual prefix):
```typescript
await myservice.sites_list({});        // Error: Tool not found
await clickup.create_task({...});    // Error: Tool not found
```

**See [references/naming_convention.md](references/naming_convention.md) for complete guide with troubleshooting.**

### Context Parameter

Many Code Mode tools require a `context` parameter (15-25 words) for analytics:

```typescript
await myservice.myservice_sites_list({
  context: "Listing sites to identify collection structure for CMS update"
});
```

This helps with usage tracking and debugging.

### Tool Name Translation

> **Note:** `list_tools()` returns names in `a.b.c` format (e.g., `myservice.myservice.sites_list`). To call the tool, use underscore format: `myservice.myservice_sites_list()`. The `tool_info()` function shows the correct calling syntax.

### Basic Workflow

1. Discover tools with `search_tools()` or `list_tools()`.
2. Confirm exact callable syntax with `tool_info()`.
3. Execute `call_tool_chain()` with `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}` calls.
4. Return structured state from the TypeScript block.

Full multi-tool examples live in [references/workflows.md](references/workflows.md).

---

## 4. PROJECT CONFIGURATION

### Two MCP Configuration Systems

**IMPORTANT**: Code Mode only accesses tools in `.utcp_config.json`. Native MCP tools are NOT accessed through Code Mode.

**1. Native MCP** (`opencode.json`) - Direct tools (call directly, NOT through Code Mode):
- **Sequential Thinking**: `sequential_thinking_sequentialthinking()`
- **Spec Kit continuity + memory**: `/speckit:resume` for recovery; native Spec Kit Memory tools such as `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, `memory_context()`, and `memory_search()` when canonical packet sources need deeper support
- **Code Mode server**: The Code Mode tool itself
- **Note**: Some AI environments have built-in extended thinking capabilities that may supersede Sequential Thinking MCP.

**2. Code Mode MCP** (`.utcp_config.json`) - External tools accessed through Code Mode:
- **MCP Config**: `.utcp_config.json` (project root)
- **Environment Variables**: `.env` (project root)
- **External tools**: MyService, Figma, Chrome DevTools, ClickUp, Notion, etc.
- These are accessed via `call_tool_chain()` wrapper

### How to Discover Available Code Mode Tools

**These discovery methods ONLY work for Code Mode tools in `.utcp_config.json`**
**They do NOT show Sequential Thinking (which is in `.mcp.json`)**

**Step 1: Check Configuration**
```typescript
// Read .utcp_config.json to see configured Code Mode MCP servers
// Look for "manual_call_templates" array
// Each object has a "name" field (this is the manual name)
// Check "disabled" field - if true, server is not active

// NOTE: Sequential Thinking is NOT in this file
// Sequential Thinking is in .mcp.json and called directly
```

**Step 2: Use Progressive Discovery**
```typescript
// Search for Code Mode tools by description
const tools = await search_tools({
  task_description: "browser automation",
  limit: 10
});

// List all available Code Mode tools
const allTools = await list_tools();

// Get info about a specific Code Mode tool
const info = await tool_info({
  tool_name: "server_name.server_name_tool_name"
});

// NOTE: These discovery tools are part of Code Mode
// They only show tools configured in .utcp_config.json
// Sequential Thinking will NOT appear in these results
```

### Critical Naming Convention (Code Mode Tools Only)

**See Section 3: Critical Naming Pattern for the complete guide.**

**Quick reminder**: `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}` (e.g., `myservice.myservice_sites_list()`)

**Sequential Thinking Exception**:
- NOT in `.utcp_config.json` - uses native MCP tools
- Call directly: `sequential_thinking_sequentialthinking()`
- Does NOT use `call_tool_chain()`
- Sequential Thinking MCP provides structured reasoning for complex multi-step problems.

### Configuration Structure

Use `.utcp_config.json` with `manual_call_templates[]`; each entry defines the manual name, MCP server command/args/env, and disabled state. See [references/configuration.md](references/configuration.md) and [assets/config_template.md](assets/config_template.md).

### Critical: Prefixed Environment Variables

> **⚠️ IMPORTANT**: Code Mode prefixes ALL environment variables with `{manual_name}_` from your configuration.

**Example:**
- Config has `"name": "clickup"` and env section references `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}`
- Your `.env` file MUST use: `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_xxx`
- Using `CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_xxx` will cause: `Error: Variable 'clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY' not found`

**Quick Reference:**

| Manual Name | Config Reference | .env Variable |
|-------------|-----------------|---------------|
| `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` |
| `figma` | `${FIGMA_API_KEY}` | `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` |
| `notion` | `${NOTION_TOKEN}` | `notion_NOTION_TOKEN` |

See [env_template.md](assets/env_template.md) for complete examples.

### How to Check Active Code Mode Servers

**IMPORTANT**: This only shows Code Mode servers in `.utcp_config.json`, NOT Sequential Thinking

Run `list_tools()` through Code Mode and group returned names by the prefix before the first dot. Sequential Thinking is native MCP and will not appear.

---

## 5. RULES

### ✅ ALWAYS

- **Use Code Mode for ALL MCP tool calls** - Mandatory for ClickUp, Notion, Figma, MyService, Chrome DevTools, etc.
- **Follow naming pattern**: `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}` (see [naming_convention.md](references/naming_convention.md))
- **Use progressive discovery**: `search_tools()` before calling unknown tools
- **Use try/catch** for error handling in multi-step workflows
- **Set appropriate timeouts**: 30s (simple), 60s (complex), 120s+ (very complex)
- **Console.log progress** in complex workflows for debugging

<content>
201: ### Official ClickUp MCP (Secondary Path)
202: 
203: **Prerequisites:**
204: - Code Mode MCP configured in opencode.json (see INSTALL_GUIDE.md)
205: - `CLICKUP_API_KEY` and `CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` set in env block
206: 
207: **Configuration** (add to `opencode.json` mcpServers):
208: ```json
209: "clickup": {
210:   "command": "npx",
211:   "args": ["-y", "@clickup/mcp-server"],
212:   "env": {
213:     "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "pk_YOUR_TOKEN",
214:     "CLICKUP_TEAM_ID": "YOUR_WORKSPACE_ID"
215:   }
216: }
217: ```
218: 
219: **Invocation via Code Mode:**
220: ```typescript
221: // Tool naming: clickup.clickup_{tool_name}
222: const result = await call_tool_chain([
223:   {
224:     tool: "clickup.clickup_create_document",
225:     input: {
226:       name: "Sprint Notes",
227:       parent: { type: 4, id: "LIST_ID" },
228:       content: "# Sprint Notes\n\n..."
229:     }
230:   }
231: ]);
232: ```
233: 
234: **When to prefer MCP:**
235: - Creating or reading ClickUp Documents
236: - Managing Goals and OKRs
237: - Bulk-creating 5+ tasks simultaneously
238: - Webhooks, custom views, user groups
239: - Enterprise features (audit logs, guests)
240: 
241: **Limitations:**
242: - No dry-run for task completion (always specify correct status)
243: - No offline mode
244: - Requires Code Mode MCP to be configured
245: 
246: ---
247: 
248: ## 4. RULES
249: 
250: ### ALWAYS
251: 
252: 1. **Run `cupt statuses <id>` before any `cupt done` call** — each ClickUp list has its own status schema; the closed status varies (Done, Complete, Closed, etc.). Never assume.
253: 2. **Use `cupt done <id> --dry-run` before batch completion** — verify resolved status for every task before writing. One dry-run per task in a batch loop.
254: 3. **Use `--json` flag for all cupt read commands** when processing output programmatically: `cupt list --json`, `cupt show <id> --json`, etc.
255: 4. **Run `cupt --version && cupt status` as preflight** before starting a ClickUp workflow session.
256: 5. **Treat empty `cupt list` results as valid** — an empty queue is not an error. Before escalating: check tag spelling, try `--all` flag, verify team name via `cupt teams`.
257: 6. **Use `cupt context <id>`** before acting on a task to understand its parent and sibling relationships.
258: 
259: ### NEVER
260: 
261: 1. **Never hardcode status names across tasks** — `"Done"` in one list may not exist in another. Use `cupt statuses <id>` to discover the correct status for each task's list.
262: 2. **Never run `cupt done` on multiple tasks without per-task dry-run first** — batch status errors are hard to reverse.
263: 3. **Never use `@krodak/clickup-cli` (`cu` command)** — this is a different tool not supported by this skill. The `cu` binary also conflicts with the system `cu` command on some Unix systems.

### Official ClickUp MCP (Secondary Path)

**Prerequisites:**
- Code Mode MCP configured in opencode.json (see INSTALL_GUIDE.md)
- `CLICKUP_API_KEY` and `CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` set in env block

**Configuration** (add to `opencode.json` mcpServers):
```json
"clickup": {
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "@clickup/mcp-server"],
  "env": {
    "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "pk_YOUR_TOKEN",
    "CLICKUP_TEAM_ID": "YOUR_WORKSPACE_ID"
  }
}
```

**Invocation via Code Mode:**
```typescript

display.text:
### Official ClickUp MCP (Secondary Path)

**Prerequisites:**
- Code Mode MCP configured in opencode.json (see INSTALL_GUIDE.md)
- `CLICKUP_API_KEY` and `CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` set in env block

**Configuration** (add to `opencode.json` mcpServers):
```json
"clickup": {
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "@clickup/mcp-server"],
  "env": {
    "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "pk_YOUR_TOKEN",
    "CLICKUP_TEAM_ID": "YOUR_WORKSPACE_ID"
  }
}
```

**Invocation via Code Mode:**
```typescript
// Tool naming: clickup.clickup_{tool_name}
const result = await call_tool_chain([
  {
    tool: "clickup.clickup_create_document",
    input: {
      name: "Sprint Notes",
      parent: { type: 4, id: "LIST_ID" },
      content: "# Sprint Notes\n\n..."
    }
  }
]);
```

**When to prefer MCP:**
- Creating or reading ClickUp Documents
- Managing Goals and OKRs
- Bulk-creating 5+ tasks simultaneously
- Webhooks, custom views, user groups
- Enterprise features (audit logs, guests)

**Limitations:**
- No dry-run for task completion (always specify correct status)
- No offline mode
- Requires Code Mode MCP to be configured

---

## 4. RULES

### ALWAYS

1. **Run `cupt statuses <id>` before any `cupt done` call** — each ClickUp list has its own status schema; the closed status varies (Done, Complete, Closed, etc.). Never assume.
2. **Use `cupt done <id> --dry-run` before batch completion** — verify resolved status for every task before writing. One dry-run per task in a batch loop.
3. **Use `--json` flag for all cupt read commands** when processing output programmatically: `cupt list --json`, `cupt show <id> --json`, etc.
4. **Run `cupt --version && cupt status` as preflight** before starting a ClickUp workflow session.
5. **Treat empty `cupt list` results as valid** — an empty queue is not an error. Before escalating: check tag spelling, try `--all` flag, verify team name via `cupt teams`.
6. **Use `cupt context <id>`** before acting on a task to understand its parent and sibling relationships.

### NEVER

1. **Never hardcode status names across tasks** — `"Done"` in one list may not exist in another. Use `cupt statuses <id>` to discover the correct status for each task's list.
2. **Never run `cupt done` on multiple tasks without per-task dry-run first** — batch status errors are hard to reverse.
3. **Never use `@krodak/clickup-cli` (`cu` command)** — this is a different tool not supported by this skill. The `cu` binary also conflicts with the system `cu` command on some Unix systems.
4. **Never auto-modify `opencode.json`** — print MCP config snippets for user to apply; never write to config files programmatically.
5. **Never fabricate tasks** — if `cupt list` returns empty, the queue is genuinely empty. Report this clearly.
6. **Never use the MCP for daily task ops** — cupt handles these more efficiently and with dry-run safety.

### ESCALATE IF

- cupt is not installed and `scripts/install.sh` fails → report Python version and pip issues
- `cupt status` shows auth failure → direct to `cupt auth` or `cupt config --api-token`
- `cupt list --team X` is extremely slow (>30s) → team filter is client-side on large workspaces; suggest combining with `--tag` to reduce result set
- MCP connection fails → verify `CLICKUP_API_KEY` env var and `CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` in opencode.json
- Task status after `cupt done` is unexpected → run `cupt statuses <id>` and report available statuses

---

## 5. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] `cupt --version` prints version string
- [ ] `cupt status` shows workspace name without error
- [ ] `cupt list --today --json` returns valid JSON array (even if empty)
- [ ] `cupt statuses <id>` returns status list for the task's list
- [ ] Dry-run before batch: `cupt done <id> --dry-run` shows resolved status
- [ ] For MCP operations: Code Mode `clickup.clickup_get_workspace` returns workspace data

---

## 6. INTEGRATION POINTS

**Gate 2 (Skill Routing):** This skill activates at ≥0.8 confidence for ClickUp task management requests. The skill advisor matches on: `clickup`, `cupt`, `task management`, `work queue`, `time tracking`, `mark done`.

**Code Mode MCP:** Official ClickUp MCP tools are invoked via `mcp__code_mode__call_tool_chain`. Tool naming convention: `clickup.clickup_{tool_name}`. See references/mcp_tools.md for the full tool catalog.

**Memory:** Save ClickUp workflow context (current list, active tags, workspace ID) using `/memory:save` when switching sessions.

**Tool Usage:** Use Bash for cupt CLI commands. Use mcp__code_mode__call_tool_chain for official MCP operations. Use Read to load references on demand.

---

## 7. QUICK REFERENCE

### cupt Command Cheat Sheet

| Category | Command | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| **Auth** | `cupt auth` | Interactive authentication wizard |
| | `cupt config --api-token pk_xxx` | Set Personal API Token directly |
| | `cupt status` | Show auth status + workspace |
| | `cupt logout` | Clear stored credentials |
| **Config** | `cupt config --workspace-id <id>` | Set default workspace |
| | `cupt config --default-list <id>` | Set default list |
| | `cupt config --show` | Display current configuration |
| **Tasks** | `cupt list` | List assigned tasks |
| | `cupt list --today` | Tasks due today |
| | `cupt list --week` | Tasks due this week |
| | `cupt list --overdue` | Overdue tasks |
| | `cupt list --tag <name>` | Filter by tag (server-side, fast) |
| | `cupt list --team <name>` | Filter by team (client-side, slow) |
| | `cupt list --all` | All tasks including team |

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: CODE MODE UTCP CONFIGURATION VALIDATOR
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
"""Code Mode UTCP Configuration Validator.

Validates .utcp_config.json files for:
- Valid JSON structure
- Valid manual names (JavaScript identifiers)
- Required fields present
- No duplicate manual names
- Environment variable references valid
- MCP server configuration completeness

Usage:
    python3 validate_config.py <path-to-.utcp_config.json>
    python3 validate_config.py <path-to-.utcp_config.json> --check-env <path-to-.env>
"""

import argparse

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: CODE MODE UTCP CONFIGURATION VALIDATOR
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
"""Code Mode UTCP Configuration Validator.

Validates .utcp_config.json files for:
- Valid JSON structure
- Valid manual names (JavaScript identifiers)
- Required fields present
- No duplicate manual names
- Environment variable references valid
- MCP server configuration completeness

Usage:
    python3 validate_config.py <path-to-.utcp_config.json>
    python3 validate_config.py <path-to-.utcp_config.json> --check-env <path-to-.env>
"""

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. CONFIGURATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

VALID_IDENTIFIER = re.compile(r'^[a-zA-Z_$][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*$')
ENV_VAR_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\$\{([A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\}')


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. VALIDATOR
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class ConfigValidator:
    """Validates Code Mode UTCP configuration files.

    Performs structural validation on .utcp_config.json files,
    checking JSON syntax, manual name conventions, required fields,
    and optionally validating environment variable references against
    a .env file.

    Args:
        config_path: Path to the .utcp_config.json file.
        env_path: Optional path to a .env file for variable validation.
    """

    def __init__(self, config_path: str, env_path: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
        self.config_path = Path(config_path)
        self.env_path = Path(env_path) if env_path else None
        self.errors: List[str] = []
        self.warnings: List[str] = []
        self.config: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        self.env_vars: Set[str] = set()
        self._required_env_vars: Set[Tuple[str, str]] = set()

    def validate(self) -> bool:
        """Run all validation checks.

        Returns:
            True if validation passed with no errors.
        """
        print(f"Validating configuration: {self.config_path}\n")

        if not self._load_config():
            return False

        if self.env_path:
            self._load_env()

        self._validate_structure()
        self._validate_manual_templates()

        if self.env_path:
            self._validate_env_vars()

        self._print_results()
        return len(self.errors) == 0

    def _load_config(self) -> bool:
        """Load and parse the JSON configuration file.

        Returns:
            True if the file was loaded and parsed successfully.
        """
        if not self.config_path.exists():
            self.errors.append(f"Configuration file not found: {self.config_path}")
            return False

        try:
            with open(self.config_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                parsed_config = json.load(f)
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            self.errors.append(f"Invalid JSON: {e}")
            return False
        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
            self.errors.append(f"Unable to read configuration file: {e}")
            return False

        if not isinstance(parsed_config, dict):
            self.errors.append("Top-level JSON must be an object")
            return False

        self.config = parsed_config
        print("✓ Valid JSON structure")
        return True

    def _load_env(self) -> None:
        """Load environment variable names from a .env file.

        Parses KEY=value lines, ignoring comments and blank lines.
        """
        if not self.env_path.exists():
            self.warnings.append(f".env file not found: {self.env_path}")
            return

        try:
            with open(self.env_path, 'r') as f:
                for line in f:
                    line = line.strip()
                    if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:
                        key = line.split('=', 1)[0].strip()
                        self.env_vars.add(key)
            print(f"✓ Loaded {len(self.env_vars)} environment variables from .env")
        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
            self.warnings.append(f"Error reading .env file: {e}")

    def _validate_structure(self) -> None:
        """Validate top-level configuration structure.

        Checks for required fields and correct types for optional
        configuration sections.
        """
        required_fields = ['manual_call_templates']
        for field in required_fields:
            if field not in self.config:
                self.errors.append(f"Missing required field: {field}")

        if 'load_variables_from' in self.config:
            if not isinstance(self.config['load_variables_from'], list):
                self.errors.append("'load_variables_from' must be an array")
            else:
                for idx, loader in enumerate(self.config['load_variables_from']):
                    if not isinstance(loader, dict):
                        self.errors.append(
                            f"load_variables_from[{idx}]: must be an object"
                        )
                        continue
                    if 'variable_loader_type' not in loader:
                        self.errors.append(
                            f"load_variables_from[{idx}]: missing 'variable_loader_type'"
                        )

        if 'tool_repository' in self.config:
            if not isinstance(self.config['tool_repository'], dict):
                self.errors.append("tool_repository: must be an object")
            elif 'tool_repository_type' not in self.config['tool_repository']:
                self.errors.append("tool_repository: missing 'tool_repository_type'")

        if 'tool_search_strategy' in self.config:
            if not isinstance(self.config['tool_search_strategy'], dict):
                self.errors.append("tool_search_strategy: must be an object")
            elif 'tool_search_strategy_type' not in self.config['tool_search_strategy']:
                self.errors.append(
                    "tool_search_strategy: missing 'tool_search_strategy_type'"
                )

        if not self.errors:
            print("✓ Valid configuration structure")

    def _validate_manual_templates(self) -> None:
        """Validate the manual_call_templates array.

        Checks each template for required fields, valid naming,
        and no duplicate manual names.
        """
        if 'manual_call_templates' not in self.config:
            return

        templates = self.config['manual_call_templates']

        if not isinstance(templates, list):
            self.errors.append("'manual_call_templates' must be an array")
            return

        if len(templates) == 0:
            self.warnings.append("No manual call templates defined")
            return

        manual_names: Set[str] = set()

        for idx, template in enumerate(templates):
            if not isinstance(template, dict):
                self.errors.append(f"manual_call_templates[{idx}]: must be an object")
                continue
            self._validate_single_template(template, idx, manual_names)

        print(f"✓ Validated {len(templates)} manual call template(s)")

    def _validate_single_template(
        self, template: Dict[str, Any], idx: int, manual_names: Set[str]
    ) -> None:
        """Validate a single manual call template entry.

        Args:
            template: The template dictionary to validate.
            idx: Index position in the templates array.
            manual_names: Accumulated set of names for duplicate detection.
        """
        prefix = f"manual_call_templates[{idx}]"

        required_fields = ['name', 'call_template_type', 'config']
        for field in required_fields:
            if field not in template:
                self.errors.append(f"{prefix}: missing required field '{field}'")

1: ---
2: title: Tool Naming Convention - Complete Guide
3: description: Critical naming pattern for MCP tool calls with troubleshooting guide.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "code mode tool naming convention"
6:   - "manual name prefix pattern"
7:   - "tool not found error"
8:   - "manual underscore tool name"
9:   - "namespace dot notation"
10: importance_tier: important
11: contextType: implementation
12: ---
13: 
14: # Tool Naming Convention - Complete Guide
15: 
16: Master the critical naming pattern to avoid the #1 most common Code Mode error.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 1. OVERVIEW
21: 
22: ### Core Pattern
23: 
24: ```typescript
25: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}
26: ```
27: 
28: **Pattern breakdown:**
29: 1. **First part:** Manual name (from `.utcp_config.json`) as namespace
30: 2. **Dot separator:** Namespace accessor
31: 3. **Second part:** Manual name + underscore + tool name
32: 
33: **Why this pattern exists:**
34: - **Namespace collision prevention** - Multiple MCP servers won't conflict
35: - **Clear source identification** - You know which manual provides each tool
36: - **TypeScript interface generation** - Enables proper type checking
37: - **UTCP protocol requirement** - Not optional, breaks without it
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 2. COMMON MISTAKES
42: 
43: ### Mistake 1: Missing Manual Prefix
44: 
45: **❌ Wrong:**
46: ```typescript
47: await myservice.sites_list({});
48: await clickup.create_task({...});
49: await figma.get_file({...});
50: ```
51: 
52: **✅ Correct:**
53: ```typescript
54: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
55: await clickup.clickup_create_task({...});
56: await figma.figma_get_file({...});
57: ```
58: 
59: **Error message you'll see:**
60: ```
61: Error: Tool not found: myservice.sites_list
62: Available tools: myservice.myservice_sites_list, myservice.myservice_collections_list, ...
63: ```
64: 
65: **Fix:** Add manual prefix to tool name: `{manual}_{tool}`
66: 
67: ### Mistake 2: Wrong Dot Notation
68: 
69: **❌ Wrong:**
70: ```typescript
71: await myservice.myservice.sites_list({});
72: await clickup.clickup.create_task({...});
73: ```
74: 
75: **✅ Correct:**
76: ```typescript
77: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
78: await clickup.clickup_create_task({...});
79: ```
80: 
81: **Error message you'll see:**
82: ```
83: TypeError: myservice.myservice is not a function
84: ```
85: 
86: **Fix:** Use single dot: `manual.manual_tool` not `manual.manual.tool`
87: 
88: ### Mistake 3: CamelCase vs snake_case Confusion
89: 
90: **❌ Wrong:**
91: ```typescript
92: await myservice.myserviceSitesList({});
93: await clickup.clickUpCreateTask({...});
94: ```
95: 
96: **✅ Correct:**
97: ```typescript
98: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
99: await clickup.clickup_create_task({...});
100: ```
101: 
102: **Error message you'll see:**
103: ```
104: Error: Tool not found: myservice.myserviceSitesList
105: Did you mean: myservice.myservice_sites_list?
106: ```
107: 
108: **Fix:** Use snake_case for tool names, not camelCase
109: 
110: ### Mistake 4: Using MCP Server Name Instead of Manual Name
111: 
112: **❌ Wrong (using MCP server name from config):**
113: ```json
114: // .utcp_config.json has:
115: "mcpServers": { "myservice": {...} }
116: 
117: // But manual name is different:
118: "name": "myservice_prod"
119: ```
120: 
121: ```typescript
122: // Wrong - using MCP server name
123: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
124: ```
125: 
126: **✅ Correct (using manual name):**
127: ```typescript
128: // Correct - using manual name
129: await myservice_prod.myservice_prod_sites_list({});
130: ```
131: 
132: **Fix:** Always use the `name` field from manual_call_templates, not the mcpServers key
133: 
134: ---
135: 
136: ## 3. CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES
137: 
138: ### Example 1: MyService
139: 
140: **Configuration:**
141: ```json
142: {
143:   "name": "myservice",
144:   "call_template_type": "mcp",
145:   "config": {
146:     "mcpServers": {
147:       "myservice": {
148:         "transport": "stdio",
149:         "command": "npx",
150:         "args": ["mcp-remote", "https://mcp.myservice.com/sse"]
151:       }
152:     }
153:   }
154: }
155: ```
156: 
157: **Correct tool calls:**
158: ```typescript
159: // Site management
160: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});
161: await myservice.myservice_sites_get({ site_id: "..." });
162: await myservice.myservice_sites_publish({ site_id: "..." });
163: 
164: // Collections
165: await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: "..." });
166: await myservice.myservice_collections_get({ collection_id: "..." });
167: 
168: // CMS Items
169: await myservice.myservice_collections_items_list_items({ collection_id: "..." });
170: await myservice.myservice_collections_items_create_item_live({
171:   collection_id: "...",
172:   request: { items: [{...}] }
173: });
174: 
175: // Pages
176: await myservice.myservice_pages_list({ site_id: "..." });
177: await myservice.myservice_pages_get_metadata({ page_id: "..." });
178: 
179: // Guide/Help
180: await myservice.myservice_myservice_guide_tool({});

(Showing lines 1-180 of 561. Use offset=181 to continue.)
</content>

12: ---
13: 
14: # Configuration Guide - .utcp_config.json and Environment Setup
15: 
16: Set up Code Mode UTCP with MCP servers, environment variables, and credentials.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 1. OVERVIEW
21: 
22: ### Required Files
23: 
24: **Two files required:**
25: 
26: 1. **`.utcp_config.json`** - MCP server definitions and tool configuration
27: 2. **`.env`** - API keys, tokens, and credentials
28: 
29: **File locations:** Both should be in the same directory (typically project root)
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. CONFIGURATION STRUCTURE
34: 
35: ### Complete Example
36: 
37: ```json
38: {
39:   "load_variables_from": [
40:     {
41:       "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
42:       "env_file_path": ".env"
43:     }
44:   ],
45:   "tool_repository": {
46:     "tool_repository_type": "in_memory"
47:   },
48:   "tool_search_strategy": {
49:     "tool_search_strategy_type": "tag_and_description_word_match"
50:   },
51:   "manual_call_templates": [
52:     {
53:       "name": "myservice",
54:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
55:       "config": {
56:         "mcpServers": {
57:           "myservice": {
58:             "transport": "stdio",
59:             "command": "npx",
60:             "args": ["mcp-remote", "https://mcp.myservice.com/sse"],
61:             "env": {}
62:           }
63:         }
64:       }
65:     },
66:     {
67:       "name": "clickup",
68:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
69:       "config": {
70:         "mcpServers": {
71:           "clickup": {
72:             "transport": "stdio",
73:             "command": "npx",
74:             "args": ["-y", "@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server"],
75:             "env": {
76:               "CLICKUP_API_KEY": "${CLICKUP_API_KEY}",
77:               "CLICKUP_TEAM_ID": "${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}"
78:             }
79:           }
80:         }
81:       }
82:     },
83:     {
84:       "name": "figma",
85:       "call_template_type": "mcp",
86:       "config": {
87:         "mcpServers": {
88:           "figma": {
89:             "transport": "stdio",
90:             "command": "npx",
91:             "args": ["-y", "figma-developer-mcp", "--stdio"],
92:             "env": {
93:               "FIGMA_API_KEY": "${FIGMA_API_KEY}"
94:             }
95:           }
96:         }
97:       }
98:     }
99:   ]
100: }
101: ```
102: 
103: ---
104: 
105: ## 3. CONFIGURATION ELEMENTS
106: 
107: ### 1. Variable Loading
108: 
109: ```json
110: "load_variables_from": [
111:   {
112:     "variable_loader_type": "dotenv",
113:     "env_file_path": ".env"
114:   }
115: ]
116: ```
117: 
118: **Purpose:** Load environment variables from `.env` file
119: 
120: **Options:**
121: - `"variable_loader_type": "dotenv"` - Load from .env file
122: - `"variable_loader_type": "system"` - Load from system environment
123: 
124: **File path:**
125: - Relative to `.utcp_config.json` location
126: - Can be absolute path if needed
127: 
128: ### 2. Tool Repository
129: 
130: ```json
131: "tool_repository": {
132:   "tool_repository_type": "in_memory"
133: }
134: ```
135: 
136: **Purpose:** Define where tool schemas are stored
137: 
138: **Options:**
139: - `"in_memory"` - Store in memory (recommended, faster)
140: - `"file"` - Store in file system (persistent)
141: 
142: **Recommendation:** Use `"in_memory"` for best performance
143: 
144: ### 3. Tool Search Strategy
145: 
146: ```json
147: "tool_search_strategy": {
148:   "tool_search_strategy_type": "tag_and_description_word_match"
149: }
150: ```
151: 
152: **Purpose:** Define how `search_tools()` finds tools
153: 
154: **Options:**
155: - `"tag_and_description_word_match"` - Keyword matching (fast, simple)
156: - `"semantic"` - Semantic search (slower, more accurate)
157: - `"exact"` - Exact name matching only
158: 
159: **Recommendation:** Use `"tag_and_description_word_match"` for balance of speed and accuracy
160: 
161: ### 4. Manual Call Templates
162: 
163: **Structure:**
164: 
165: ```json
166: "manual_call_templates": [
167:   {
168:     "name": "manual_name",           // TypeScript namespace
169:     "call_template_type": "mcp",      // Integration type
170:     "config": {                       // Type-specific config
171:       "mcpServers": {
172:         "server_key": {
173:           "transport": "stdio",
174:           "command": "npx",
175:           "args": ["package-name"],
176:           "env": {
177:             "VAR_NAME": "${ENV_VAR}"
178:           }
179:         }
180:       }
181:     }
182:   }
183: ]
184: ```
185: 
186: **Key fields explained:**
187: 
188: #### `name` Field
189: 
190: - **Purpose:** TypeScript namespace for tool calls
191: - **Usage:** `await {name}.{name}_{tool}(...)`
192: - **Rules:**
193:   - Must be valid JavaScript identifier
194:   - No hyphens, spaces, or special characters
195:   - Use underscores for word separation
196:   - Case-sensitive
197: 
198: **Examples:**
199: ```json
200: "name": "myservice"           // ✅ Good
201: "name": "clickup"           // ✅ Good
202: "name": "chrome_devtools_1" // ✅ Good (with instance number)
203: "name": "myservice-api"       // ❌ Bad (hyphens not allowed)
204: "name": "my server"         // ❌ Bad (spaces not allowed)
205: ```
206: 
207: #### `call_template_type` Field
208: 
209: **Options:**
210: 
211: 1. **`"mcp"`** - MCP server integration (most common)
212:    ```json
213:    {
214:      "call_template_type": "mcp",
215:      "config": {
216:        "mcpServers": { /* MCP config */ }
217:      }
218:    }
219:    ```
220: 

(Showing lines 1-220 of 770. Use offset=221 to continue.)
</content>
